summary: Canonical bar-chart dashboard card with shared data-viz chrome, a filter action, a fill chart region, and an optional static legend.
useWhen: A dashboard widget needs a regular, stacked, or percentage bar chart in the standard data-viz card.
A stacked bar chart needs a visible static series key beneath the chart.
avoidWhen: The visualization needs selectable legend rows or chart-to-legend hover synchronization.
The product needs supporting metrics or an alternate chart layout beyond the chart and optional legend.

alternatives.when: The bar chart needs a custom layout without card chrome.
alternatives.reason: Compose ChartBar directly in the product-owned layout.

alternatives.when: The visualization communicates change as continuous lines.
alternatives.reason: CardDataVizLine owns the canonical line-chart card layout.

alternatives.when: The visualization communicates parts of one total with synchronized slice emphasis.
alternatives.reason: CardDataVizDonut owns the donut layout and bidirectional hover synchronization.

accessibility: Provide a concise heading that identifies the measure and meaningful visible labels for every legend entry.
Keep essential series and value context available textually rather than relying on bar color alone.
The card disables decorative legend-row hover; genuine legend links retain native link semantics and affordances.
states: The chart region fills the remaining card body while an optional legend remains content-sized.
The card disables local hover highlighting on a slotted ChartLegend because bar legends are static keys.
Chart and legend regions render only when their corresponding slots contain content, avoiding empty region spacing.
The owning product controls data, filtering, loading, empty, and error state; the card emits the filter action only.
responsiveBehavior: The selected sm, md, or lg shell size controls the card dimensions while the bar chart shrinks within its chart region.
The horizontal legend wraps complete entries when the available width is constrained.
The parent dashboard owns card wrapping and breakpoint changes.

customElements: Assign data or series and categories as JavaScript properties, then place the bar chart and optional legend in their named slots.
react: Keep filter and dataset state in the owner; pass the chart and optional legend with chart and legend slot attributes.
angular: Keep filter and dataset state in the owner; project the chart and optional legend into the chart and legend slots.
